prd_ref: PRD: VibePilot Dashboard Rebrand — vibeflow → vibepilot
planning_principles: Isolation-first: Each slice operates on distinct file sets with no overlap
Cascade prevention: No cross-slice code dependencies
Text-only changes: Zero visual or functional modifications

slices.name: Dashboard UI (React)
slices.description: Updates the React dashboard component brand text from Vibeflow to VibePilot
slices.boundaries: Does NOT touch terminal dashboard, documentation, or configuration files. Only affects React component at ~/vibeflow/apps/dashboard/components/MissionHeader.tsx

tasks.title: Replace Vibeflow brand text in MissionHeader.tsx
tasks.purpose: Primary rebrand: Update the dashboard header to display VibePilot instead of Vibeflow
objectives: Replace 'Vibeflow' with 'VibePilot' in the span element
Preserve all CSS classes and attributes exactly
Verify no other occurrences exist in the file
tasks.prompt_packet: CONTEXT: You are updating the brand name in a React TypeScript component. The file is at ~/vibeflow/apps/dashboard/components/MissionHeader.tsx. Line 308 currently contains: Vibeflow. This is a TEXT-ONLY change with zero visual modifications required. WHAT TO BUILD: Change the text content from 'Vibeflow' to 'VibePilot' on line 308. The CSS class 'mission-header__brand' must remain unchanged. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: 1. Line 308 reads: VibePilot 2. All other code remains identical 3. No changes to imports, types, or logic 4. File builds successfully with 'npm run build' OUTPUT FORMAT: Confirm the exact line that was changed and provide the git diff output.
tasks.routing_flag_reason: Touches existing file, needs precise line editing, requires git verification
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Single line text replacement with exact location known. No logic changes, no dependencies, trivial verification.

tasks.title: Build verification for React dashboard
tasks.purpose: Ensure the text change does not break the build
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: You are verifying that a single-line text change in MissionHeader.tsx does not break the React dashboard build. The change was from 'Vibeflow' to 'VibePilot' on line 308.

WHAT TO BUILD: Run the build process and verify it succeeds.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. npm run build completes with exit code 0
2. No TypeScript compilation errors
3. No linting errors
4. Build artifacts are generated

OUTPUT FORMAT: Provide the build command output (last 20 lines) and confirm success.
tasks.routing_flag_reason: Needs codebase access to run build commands, depends on prior file modification
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Text-only change cannot break TypeScript types. Build verification is standard procedure.

tasks.title: Final grep validation for React dashboard
tasks.purpose: Confirm zero remaining vibeflow occurrences in dashboard directory
objectives: Report any remaining occurrences if found
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: Final validation step for the React dashboard rebrand. Need to confirm no 'vibeflow' text remains in the dashboard directory.

WHAT TO BUILD: Execute a comprehensive grep search and report results.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. rg -i 'vibeflow' ~/vibeflow/apps/dashboard/ returns zero results
2. If any results found, list them with file paths and line numbers
3. Confirm 'VibePilot' appears exactly once in MissionHeader.tsx

OUTPUT FORMAT: Provide the grep command and its output (or 'No matches found').
tasks.routing_flag_reason: Needs filesystem access to run ripgrep, but simple validation only
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Simple grep command with clear pass/fail criteria. No ambiguity.

slices.name: Documentation Files
slices.description: Updates all markdown documentation files to use vibepilot instead of vibeflow
slices.boundaries: Does NOT touch code files, JSON files, or the React dashboard. Only markdown content in docs/ and config/ directories.

tasks.title: Update docs/ directory markdown files
tasks.purpose: Replace all vibeflow occurrences with vibepilot in documentation
objectives: Find all .md files in docs/ containing 'vibeflow' (case insensitive)
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: You are updating brand references in markdown documentation files. There are approximately 34 markdown files in the docs/ directory, with roughly 60+ occurrences of 'vibeflow' to replace. This is a bulk text replacement task.

WHAT TO BUILD: Use sed or a Python script to replace all case-insensitive occurrences of 'vibeflow' with 'vibepilot' (preserving case: Vibeflow→VibePilot, vibeflow→vibepilot, VIBEFLOW→VIBE PILOT).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. All .md files in docs/ processed
2. Case-preserving replacement applied
3. File structure and formatting unchanged
4. grep -ri 'vibeflow' docs/ returns empty

OUTPUT FORMAT: List all files modified with count of replacements per file.
tasks.routing_flag_reason: Multiple file modifications, needs filesystem access for batch processing
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Standard find-and-replace operation. Case-preserving logic is well-established. Low complexity.

tasks.title: Update config/prompts/ markdown files
tasks.purpose: Replace vibeflow with vibepilot in prompt configuration files
objectives: Verify config/researcher_context.md if it contains references
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: Update brand references in configuration prompt files located in config/prompts/ and config/researcher_context.md. These files define agent behavior and may contain multiple vibeflow references.

WHAT TO BUILD: Replace all vibeflow occurrences with vibepilot using case-preserving logic.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. All config/prompts/*.md files processed
2. config/researcher_context.md processed
3. grep -ri 'vibeflow' config/ returns empty
4. File content integrity maintained

OUTPUT FORMAT: List modified files with replacement counts.
tasks.routing_flag_reason: Multiple file modifications in config directory
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Same pattern as docs/ slice. Proven find-and-replace methodology.

tasks.title: Documentation grep validation
tasks.purpose: Final verification that no vibeflow remains in docs/ or config/
objectives: Run comprehensive grep across docs/ and config/
Report any stragglers with exact locations
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: Final validation for documentation rebrand. Must confirm 100% of vibeflow references have been replaced.

WHAT TO BUILD: Run grep commands and validate zero occurrences remain.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. grep -ri 'vibeflow' docs/ returns empty
2. grep -ri 'vibeflow' config/ returns empty
3. Any remaining occurrences documented with file:line details

OUTPUT FORMAT: Provide command outputs and confirmation status.
tasks.routing_flag_reason: Needs filesystem access for grep validation
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Simple grep validation with clear criteria. No complexity.

slices.name: Plans and Config Files
slices.description: Updates JSON plan files and other configuration files with vibepilot branding
exposes: Updated plan metadata with consistent branding
slices.boundaries: Does NOT touch markdown files (handled by documentation slice). Only JSON and other non-md config files.

tasks.title: Update plans/*.json files
tasks.purpose: Replace vibeflow with vibepilot in JSON plan files
objectives: Identify all .json files in plans/ containing vibeflow
Validate JSON remains parseable after changes
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: Update JSON plan files in the plans/ directory. Files like vibepilot_plan.json and vibepilot_prd.json may contain legacy 'vibeflow' references.

WHAT TO BUILD: Replace all vibeflow occurrences with vibepilot while preserving JSON validity.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. All plans/*.json files processed
2. JSON remains valid (can be parsed by jq or Python json module)
3. grep -i 'vibeflow' plans/*.json returns empty
4. No structural changes to JSON objects

OUTPUT FORMAT: List modified files and confirm JSON validity.
tasks.routing_flag_reason: JSON file modifications require careful handling to preserve validity
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Text replacement in JSON requires validation but is straightforward. Well-defined scope.

slices.name: Terminal Dashboard Verification
slices.description: Verifies that terminal dashboard already uses VibePilot branding
slices.boundaries: Read-only verification only. No modifications to terminal dashboard.

tasks.title: Verify terminal dashboard uses VibePilot
tasks.purpose: Confirm terminal dashboard already displays correct branding
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: Verify-only task for the terminal dashboard. According to the PRD, terminal_dashboard.py should already use 'VibePilot' branding.

WHAT TO BUILD: Run grep commands to verify the current state. No file modifications.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. grep -i 'vibepilot' dashboard/terminal_dashboard.py returns matches
2. grep -i 'vibeflow' dashboard/terminal_dashboard.py returns empty
3. Header displays 'VIBEPILOT DASHBOARD'

OUTPUT FORMAT: Provide grep output and confirm status.
tasks.routing_flag_reason: Needs filesystem access to read and grep the file
tasks.confidence_reasoning: Read-only verification task. Simple grep with no modifications.

slices.name: Cross-Cutting Validation
slices.description: Final comprehensive validation across all slices and directories
consumes: Validation results from all other slices
slices.boundaries: Read-only validation only. Depends on completion of all other slices.

tasks.title: Create and run comprehensive validation script
tasks.purpose: Final cross-cutting verification that no vibeflow remains anywhere
tasks.prompt_packet:
CONTEXT: Final validation across all project directories. Must confirm 100% brand consistency with zero vibeflow occurrences remaining.

WHAT TO BUILD: Run comprehensive grep across all relevant directories and generate a validation report.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
1. ~/vibeflow/apps/dashboard/ - 0 occurrences
2. docs/ - 0 occurrences
3. config/ - 0 occurrences
4. plans/ - 0 occurrences
5. dashboard/ - 0 occurrences
6. Any remaining occurrences documented with full context

OUTPUT FORMAT: Provide directory-by-directory grep results and final PASS/FAIL status.

touches_slices: Uses summary results from all slices
receives_from_slices: Validation summaries from dashboard-ui, documentation, plans-config, terminal-dashboard

tasks.confidence_reasoning: Aggregates results from validated slices. Simple report generation.

cross_slice_interfaces.purpose: Validation slice aggregates results from dashboard slice
Validation slice aggregates results from docs slice
Validation slice aggregates results from plans slice
Validation slice includes terminal dashboard status

notes: Each slice operates on distinct file paths with zero overlap
validation: read-only aggregation of other slice results
No code imports between slices - only summary-level dependencies
